free_text: Measurement of the neutron total cross sections of
aluminum at the back-n white neutron source of CSNS
Spallation Neutron Source Science Center,
Dongguan
State Key Laboratory of Particle Detection
and Electronics, Beijing
University of South China, Hengyang
Xian Jiaotong University, Xian
Erratum to J,EPJ/A,57,232,2021. In the original
article the name of the second author was incorrectly
written as Yiwei Zhang. His correct name is Yiwei Yang.
Back-n white neutron source of China
Spallation Neutron Source (CSNS)
Neutrons are produced via 1.6 GeV proton-
induced spallation reactions on a Tungsten target.
The incident proton pulse was in double-bunch mode,
about 41 ns (FWHM) wide and 410 ns apart.
Two thickness samples are used in the experiment to
verify the self-consistency by comparing their
results, with purity of 99.999% and density of 2.69
g/cm3. The diameters are 70.10 +- 0.02 mm and 70.09
+- 0.02 mm, the thicknesses are 40.00 +- 0.02 mm and
60.18 +- 0.02 mm, the masses are 414.97 +- 0.01 mm
A multi-layer fast fission chamber. There are
three 235U and three 238U cells in the fission chamber.
A flight path about 76 m.
Data presented by author
The data received from Xing-Yan Liu
Jimin Wang (CNDC)
40 mm thick aluminum
60 mm thick aluminum
The measured results with 235U cells
The uncertainty with 235U for 40 mm and 60
mmthick aluminum is 0.7-22.3% and 0.6-12.4% in the
energy range of 10 keV-20 MeV, respectively.
Data measured with 238U cells
40 mm thick aluminum
60 mm thick aluminum
The measured results with 238U cells
The uncertainty with 238U for 40 mm and 60
mmthick aluminum is 1.6-6.3% and 1.3-5.2% in the
energy range of 1-20 MeV, respectively.
Data measured with 235U cells
